existing_capabilities: ORP Pacing Engine (competitive core — do not rebuild)
Gesture System v4 (calibrated swipes, WPM dial, hints)
Basic App Scaffold (library, reading, parallax_reading, settings, analytics screens)
assumptions: Isar is the persistence layer for new features; existing SharedPreferences config stays
Desktop/web targets are stretch goals, not blockers for any release
pdfrx FFI constraint limits PDF page rendering to main isolate; text classification can use Isolate.run()
Solo developer capacity: 20 hours/week, max 3 concurrent agents
Test baseline: 264 pass, 20 skip, 4 pre-existing failures — do not introduce new failures
Analysis baseline: dart analyze lib/ must report zero issues

milestones.title: Pacing Engine — Best of Both Worlds
milestones.status_detail: All features complete: algorithm port, WordTimer integration with warm-up ramp, and Pacing Settings UI with three independent scale sliders.
milestones.priority_rationale: Inserted ahead of M1.3 at user request. Comparison against ionutdecebal/rsvpnano (ESP32 RSVP reader, 561 stars) revealed our uniform-interval timer is materially worse for comprehension than their per-word duration model. Their pacing algorithm is orthogonal to rendering — we can absorb it without touching ORP, 3D viewport, ContextReveal, or gestures. Highest-ROI single change available before launch.

model.rationale: Milestone-level planning that touches the competitive core (word_timer.dart) — needs strategic reasoning to avoid regressing existing pacing behavior

competitive_analysis.license: MIT — algorithm/constants may be reimplemented in Dart with attribution
competitive_analysis.their_strength: Per-word duration model: base interval (60000/WPM) + length bonus (tiered, with compound-joiner and tech-connector detection) + complexity bonus (syllable groups, all-caps, mixed alphanumeric) + punctuation pause (comma/dash/semicolon/period/exclamation/ellipsis with abbreviation suppression). Each category independently user-scalable 25-200%. 30+ host-side unit tests.
competitive_analysis.integration_strategy: DONE — WordTimerNotifier._scheduleNext() now calls durationForWord() for per-word intervals. PacingConfig persisted in AppConfig. Warm-up ramp (80%→100% over 10s) also implemented during this milestone. Rendering, gestures, ContextReveal, ORP, and 3D parallax were not touched.

epics.title: Per-Word Adaptive Pacing
epics.status_detail: All features complete: algorithm port, WordTimer integration, and Pacing Settings UI.
epics.description: Replace the uniform 60000/WPM interval in WordTimerNotifier with a per-word duration function modeled on RSVP Nano's pacing algorithm. Adds length, complexity, and punctuation bonuses to each word's display time, with independent user-scalable multipliers and abbreviation-aware sentence pause suppression. Preserves all existing rendering, A/B test conditions, drift correction, and auto-rewind behavior.

model.rationale: Touches the competitive core (word_timer.dart) — must coordinate algorithm port, settings UI, persistence, and test coverage without regressing existing OrpCondition A/B test infrastructure

guardrails: Do NOT modify lib/core/orp.dart, lib/three_d/, ContextReveal, or gesture system
Pacing engine is pure Dart — no platform channels, no isolates required (algorithm is microsecond-scale)
Existing OrpCondition A/B test wiring must continue to work unchanged
Drift correction and auto-rewind-on-resume behavior must be preserved
Settings UI uses RunThruColors and RunThruTextStyles tokens — no raw Color/TextStyle
Per-category scale sliders default to 100% (zero behavior change for existing users until they opt in)
Reduced-motion users still get pacing variation (this is timing, not animation — accessibility win, not loss)

ethical_blockers.rule: Per-word pacing improves comprehension for ADHD/neurodivergent readers — this is an accessibility feature and is NEVER paywalled per project ethical commitment
RSVP Nano is MIT-licensed. Add attribution comment in word_duration.dart and an entry to NOTICE/THIRD_PARTY.md noting algorithm constants are derived from ionutdecebal/rsvpnano.

features.title: Word Duration Algorithm Port

model.rationale: Algorithm port from C++ to Dart — mechanical translation guided by reference test suite

tasks.title: Create lib/features/reading/pacing/word_duration.dart
what_to_do: Define `PacingConfig` immutable class with: longWordDelayMs (default 200), complexWordDelayMs (default 200), punctuationDelayMs (default 200), longWordScalePercent (25-200, default 100), complexWordScalePercent (25-200, default 100), punctuationScalePercent (25-200, default 100). copyWith + ==/hashCode.
Implement `int durationForWord(String word, {required String? nextWord, required int baseIntervalMs, required PacingConfig config})` returning total ms.

Length bonus: tiered per-character percentages above 6/10/14 readable chars; compound-joiner detection ('-' between word chars → +14%); tech-connector detection ('_', '/', '.' in tokens like 'U.S.' or 'foo_bar') → +8% beyond joiners; long-compound bonus when readable >= 10 with joiner. Cap at 170%.

Complexity bonus: approximate syllable groups via vowel-group counting with silent-e adjustment ('-le'/'-ye' suffixes preserved); >2 groups → +10% per extra group, capped; all-caps token (uppercase==letterCount, letterCount>0) → +14%; mixed letter+digit → +15%; dense tech connectors → additional bonus. Cap at complexity max.

Punctuation pause: trailing-rhythm character switch — ',' → +45%, ';'/':' → +80%, '-' → +60%, '.' → +135% (suppressed if abbreviation detected), '!'/'?' → +150%, ellipsis '...' → +110%. Abbreviation detection: known list ('Mr.','Mrs.','Dr.','U.S.','etc.','i.e.','e.g.', etc.), dotted initialism (interleaved letters and dots), short-word-period heuristic (readable<=4 AND nextWord starts lowercase). Strip trailing closing quotes/parens before classifying.
Apply scale percentages: bonusMs = (bonusPercent * scalePercent / 100) * delayMs / 100. Sum length/complexity/punctuation bonus ms, add to baseIntervalMs.
All helpers are private top-level functions. Public surface: PacingConfig, durationForWord, defaultPacingConfig.
Add file-level dartdoc citing https://github.com/ionutdecebal/rsvpnano (MIT) as source of algorithm constants and tier thresholds.
Pure Dart — zero Flutter imports, zero platform channels.
acceptance_criteria: PacingConfig is immutable with copyWith, ==, hashCode
All scale fields clamp to [25, 200]; all delay fields clamp to [0, 1000]
durationForWord is pure (no side effects, deterministic)
File-level dartdoc cites RSVP Nano (MIT) attribution
Zero analysis issues

tasks.title: Port RSVP Nano test suite to Dart
what_to_do: Translate every assertion in https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ionutdecebal/rsvpnano/main/test/test_pacing/test_main.cpp into Dart group/test blocks.

Cover groups: WPM clamping (skipped — handled by WordTimerNotifier), no-bonus baseline ('a' → 200ms at 300 WPM), comma/sentence/strong-sentence/clause/dash/ellipsis pauses, sentence-pause preserved with closing quote/paren, abbreviation suppression (Mr., U.S., short-word-period before lowercase), accented/extended-Latin lowercase suppresses pause but uppercase does not, length bonus (strength → 224ms, information → 318ms), compound bonus (well-known → 264ms), all-caps complexity (NASA → 228ms), pacing scale halved (punctuation 50% → 334ms), pacing scale floor (longWord 0% → 206ms via 25% clamp).
Each test asserts exact ms equality — these are the contract.
Use `const baseInterval = 200; // 300 WPM` as the standard baseline for readability.
acceptance_criteria: All 30+ test cases from RSVP Nano's test_main.cpp ported
Every test passes against the Dart implementation
Zero new failures vs. baseline (264/20/4)

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: pure-algorithm port + its test suite is one cohesive unit — tests can be written in parallel against the documented C++ contract before Dart impl lands

features.title: WordTimer Integration

model.rationale: Integration into existing notifier — preserve drift correction, auto-rewind, A/B test wiring

tasks.title: Wire word_duration into WordTimerNotifier
what_to_do: Add `PacingConfig pacingConfig` field to WordTimerState (default: defaultPacingConfig). Add to copyWith.

Add list-of-words context to WordTimerNotifier so durationForWord can see nextWord. Currently notifier only tracks index — extend constructor or setter to accept the full word stream from the reading screen (or expose a callback `String? Function(int index) wordAt`).

In `_scheduleNext()`, replace `state.intervalMs` usage with: `final base = state.intervalMs; final word = wordAt(state.currentIndex); final next = wordAt(state.currentIndex + 1); final interval = durationForWord(word, nextWord: next, baseIntervalMs: base, config: state.pacingConfig);` — apply existing drift correction on top of `interval` (not `base`).
Persist PacingConfig via existing ConfigNotifier (lib/store/config.dart). Add JSON serialization. Migration: missing keys default to 100% scale / 200ms delay (preserves existing behavior).
OrpCondition A/B test wiring untouched — pacing is orthogonal to anchor styling.
Auto-rewind 3 words on resume: unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Existing word_timer_test.dart and orp_condition_test.dart still pass
When PacingConfig is at defaults (100%/200ms), behavior matches old uniform interval (regression test required)
Drift correction applies to the per-word interval, not the base
PacingConfig round-trips through SharedPreferences/Isar without loss
Zero analysis issues

tasks.title: Add integration test: paragraph pacing
what_to_do: Test: feed a known paragraph ('The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. NASA launched information about U.S. policy, well-known and verified.') at 300 WPM with default config. Assert total elapsed ms within ±2% of sum(durationForWord) for each word.
Test: per-category scale at 0% (clamps to 25%) reduces pause durations by exactly 75% for affected category only — other categories unaffected.
Test: WordTimerState.copyWith(pacingConfig: ...) propagates without resetting currentIndex or isPlaying.
acceptance_criteria: Three tests pass
No new failures in baseline

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: integration into the live notifier requires careful coordination with persistence and existing A/B test infrastructure — separate from the pure-algorithm port

features.title: Pacing Settings UI
features.status_detail: PacingPanel with three sliders (25-200%), live preview, reset button, and accessible info sheet. All design tokens used. Touch targets >=48dp.

model.rationale: Settings screen extension using existing design system tokens

tasks.title: Add Word Pacing settings panel
tasks.status_detail: PacingPanel widget created at lib/features/settings/widgets/pacing_panel.dart with three scale sliders, live preview, reset button, and info sheet. Wired into settings_screen.dart.
what_to_do: Create `PacingPanel` ConsumerWidget showing three sliders (Long words, Complexity, Punctuation), each 25-200%, default 100%, with current % displayed and a numeric label fallback (no color-only signal).
Add 'Reset pacing' button that restores all three to 100%.
Add a live preview row at top: a fixed sample paragraph rendered with current pacing settings via a tiny in-page playback (or a static computed-total-ms readout: 'Sample paragraph: 4.2s at 300 WPM').
Wire to ConfigNotifier so changes persist immediately.
Use RunThruColors and RunThruTextStyles tokens. Touch targets ≥48dp. Slider thumbs labeled. Semantics widgets on every interactive element.

Add an 'About pacing' info button that opens a sheet explaining: 'Long words, complex words, and punctuation get extra time. Set each to 0% (treated as 25% minimum) to nearly disable, or 200% to emphasize. This is not speed-reading — it's giving every word the time it deserves.' — frames it accessibly, no ableist language.
acceptance_criteria: Three sliders for length/complexity/punctuation scales (25-200%)
Reset button restores defaults
Settings persist via ConfigNotifier
All tokens from design system (no raw Color/TextStyle/BoxDecoration)
Touch targets ≥48dp
Screen-reader accessible (Semantics on sliders + buttons)
Info copy avoids ableist language and dark patterns
Zero analysis issues

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: settings UI is pure widget work using established design tokens — independent of algorithm and integration concerns

parallel_clusters.rationale: Algorithm port and its ported test suite can be written in parallel — tests are derived from the public C++ contract, not the in-progress Dart impl

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: per-word adaptive pacing requires a pure-algorithm port (F1.2.5.1.1), integration into the live word timer notifier preserving drift/rewind/A-B-test behavior (F1.2.5.1.2), and a settings UI for the three independent scale knobs (F1.2.5.1.3) — three concerns, three features, sequenced because integration depends on the algorithm and settings UI depends on the persisted config

completion_criteria: flutter test → no new failures vs baseline (264/20/4)
All 30+ ported RSVP Nano pacing assertions pass
NOTICE or THIRD_PARTY.md updated with RSVP Nano attribution
milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: pacing engine improvement is a single high-ROI epic with three tightly-coupled features (algorithm, integration, UI) — does not warrant being split across multiple milestones, but is foundational enough to interrupt M1.3 because launching MVP with a uniform-interval timer when a peer-validated alternative exists is a missed competitive moat

acceptance_criteria: Fenced code blocks (```lang ... ```) are stripped entirely from output
Indented code blocks (4+ leading spaces after a blank line) are stripped entirely
Markdown tables (|---|---| patterns with pipe-delimited rows) are stripped entirely
Escape sequences (\*, \[, \]) have backslash removed, character preserved
Footnote markers ([^1]) and definitions ([^1]: text) are stripped
Existing markdown stripping (headings, bold, italic, links, lists, blockquotes) unchanged
dart analyze reports zero issues

acceptance_criteria: Test: fenced code block with language tag produces zero code words in output
Test: fenced code block without language tag is stripped
Test: multi-line fenced code block (10+ lines of Python/JS) fully removed
Test: markdown table with 3+ columns and 5+ rows fully removed
Test: indented code block after blank line is removed
Test: escaped characters (\*, \[, \]) produce unescaped character
Test: real-world ChatGPT response with mixed prose+code produces only prose words
Test: real-world Claude response with markdown formatting produces clean reading tokens
Test: existing markdown tests still pass (no regressions)
All tests pass

epics.title: Instapaper Integration

model.rationale: API integration with existing content pipeline — single-scope feature work

guardrails: OAuth tokens in secure storage only (flutter_secure_storage). Never log credentials.
No silent network calls. User confirms before any fetch.
Privacy: article content stays on device after fetch. No re-upload.
No ableist language in UI copy

logout() clears tokens from secure storage and resets state
On app start, checks for existing tokens and restores session

acceptance_criteria: Displays full paragraph with comfortable line spacing (≥1.5)
Auto-advances to next paragraph after calculated reading time (based on word count and WPM)
Progress indicator shows time remaining (not color-only — uses position + label)
Tap pauses auto-advance timer
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: paragraph mode is one new widget file combining timed advance, progress indicator, and line spacing — independent rendering model from sentence mode

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: paragraph mode widget is one cohesive UI component — timer, progress, and layout are tightly coupled within one widget file

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: paragraph mode has timed auto-advance logic absent from sentence mode — requires different timer, progress indicator, and line-spacing strategy

features.title: Mode Switching

stories.title: Seamless switching between RSVP, sentence, and paragraph modes

tasks.title: Create mode switcher provider and gesture handler
acceptance_criteria: ReadingModeProvider tracks current mode (rsvp, sentence, paragraph)
Mode switch preserves current position in text
Transition between modes uses crossfade animation (respects reduced motion)
Mode switcher touch target ≥48dp
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: mode switcher combines one provider file and one widget file — tightly coupled pair that must be implemented together to avoid orphan state

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: mode switcher combines one provider and one widget in tight coordination — splitting would create circular dependency

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: mode switching is a coordination layer that depends on both sentence and paragraph mode views existing — cannot implement until F1.4.2.1 and F1.4.2.2 complete

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: multi-mode reading introduces two new widget trees (sentence view, paragraph view) plus mode-switching state — depends on E1.4.1 establishing gesture and timer patterns

epics.title: Accessibility Foundations
epics.description: Adaptive letter/word spacing, reading ruler/focus line, no color-only signals, shape/label/position fallbacks

features.title: Adaptive Spacing Controls

stories.title: User-adjustable letter and word spacing

tasks.title: Create adaptive spacing provider and settings UI
acceptance_criteria: SpacingProvider stores letter spacing (0.0-4.0) and word spacing (0.0-8.0)
Settings UI shows slider controls with current value labels
Spacing applies to all reading modes (RSVP, sentence, paragraph)
Feature is FREE (not behind any paywall check)
Persisted across app restarts via SharedPreferences
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: spacing provider is one file with slider state and SharedPreferences persistence — parallelizable with reading ruler overlay

tasks.title: Create reading ruler/focus line overlay
acceptance_criteria: Reading ruler overlays a tinted bar above and below current line
Ruler position tracks current reading position
Ruler can be toggled on/off from reading screen
Feature is FREE (not behind any paywall check)
Ruler uses semi-transparent overlay (not full opacity — maintains readability)
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: reading ruler is one overlay widget file — parallelizable with spacing provider, consumes spacing values via ref.watch

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: spacing settings provider and reading ruler overlay — independent UI surfaces (settings screen vs reading screen) enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: adaptive spacing combines a settings provider (settings screen) with a reading ruler overlay (reading screen) — two distinct UI surfaces with independent file scope

ethical_blockers.rule: Accessibility features (adaptive letter/word spacing, reading ruler, focus line) are NEVER paywalled — free forever per ethical commitment

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: accessibility foundations span settings providers, reading overlays, and design tokens — parallelizable with E1.4.1 because file scopes do not overlap

epics.title: Artifact Suppression
epics.description: Long-press suppression during pause, range select, per-source profiles, auto-detect tables/figures/code

model.rationale: Content classification system requires isolate strategy

features.title: Artifact Detection Engine

stories.title: Auto-detect tables, figures, and code blocks in content

tasks.title: Create artifact classifier that detects non-prose content in Isolate.run()
acceptance_criteria: ArtifactClassifier identifies tables (tab-separated or pipe-delimited patterns)
Identifies code blocks (indentation patterns, common syntax markers)
Identifies figure references (Figure X, Fig. X patterns)
Classification runs in Isolate.run() — verified by function signature accepting SendPort or returning Future
Returns List<ArtifactRegion> with start/end indices and type
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: artifact classifier is one new Dart file with pattern-matching logic wrapped in Isolate.run() — pure function, testable without UI

tasks.title: Add unit tests for artifact classification
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover: text with tables, text with code blocks, text with figure refs, mixed content
Tests verify correct region boundaries (±2 characters tolerance)
All tests pass
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: classifier test file covers table/code/figure detection patterns — parallelizable with implementation via expected classification contract

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: artifact classifier implementation and its test file — implementation and verification parallelizable

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: artifact detection is pure classification logic running in Isolate.run() — no UI dependency, testable in complete isolation from suppression overlay

features.title: Suppression UI

stories.title: Long-press suppression and range selection during pause

tasks.title: Create long-press gesture handler for manual artifact suppression
acceptance_criteria: Long-press during pause activates suppression mode
User can select word range to suppress
Suppressed regions shown with strikethrough + muted opacity (not color-only)
Suppression persisted per-document
Touch targets ≥48dp on selection handles
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: suppression overlay combines gesture handler and visual feedback in one widget — tightly coupled long-press + range-select interaction, not decomposable

stories.granularity_rationale: Single-task story: suppression overlay is one gesture-driven widget — long-press, range select, and visual feedback are tightly coupled in one component

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: suppression UI requires gesture handling (long-press, range select) and overlay rendering — depends on classification engine output format from F1.4.4.1

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: artifact suppression combines an isolate-based classification engine with a gesture-driven suppression UI overlay — depends on E1.3.2 content pipeline for standardized input format

parallel_clusters.rationale: RSVP polish and accessibility foundations touch different files and can proceed in parallel
Warm-up implementation and tests can be written in parallel

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: reading experience polish layers on top of M1.2 design system and M1.3 content pipeline — cannot start without styled ConsumerWidget screens and importable content

milestones.title: Launch Readiness

model.rationale: Launch milestone requires coordinating store presence with feature completeness

epics.title: Stats + Analytics Foundation
epics.description: Session time, words read, sessions completed; on-device only
guardrails: No cloud upload without per-session opt-in

features.title: Session Tracking Provider

stories.title: Track reading session metrics on-device

tasks.title: Create session stats provider with Isar persistence
acceptance_criteria: SessionStatsProvider tracks: session duration, words read, WPM average, completion percentage
ReadingSession model stored in Isar with timestamp and document reference
Data stored on-device only — no network calls
Provider auto-disposes when reading screen is left
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: session stats provider with Isar ReadingSession model is one provider + one model file — tightly coupled data layer pair changing together

tasks.title: Create analytics dashboard widget for stats screen
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows: total sessions, total words read, average session duration, streak count
All data indicators use shape/label/position (not color-only)
Uses RunThruColors and RunThruTypography tokens
Touch targets ≥48dp on any interactive stat elements
dart analyze reports zero issues
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: stats dashboard widget is one file rendering aggregated session data — parallelizable with provider creation via agreed data model

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Isar stats provider (data layer) and dashboard widget (presentation) — independent layers enable parallel execution

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: session tracking combines Isar persistence (data layer model + provider) with dashboard widget (presentation layer) — both co-located in features/stats/

ethical_blockers.rule: All analytics data stored on-device only — no cloud upload without explicit per-session opt-in

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: stats foundation requires coordinated Isar schema design, Riverpod provider, and dashboard widget across the new features/stats/ directory

epics.title: App Store Presence
epics.description: Screenshots, description, ASO, privacy policy, TestFlight/Play Store internal testing
guardrails: Privacy policy must reflect on-device-only data
No dark patterns in store listing

ethical_blockers.rule: No ableist language in store listing or screenshots

features.title: Store Listing Content

stories.title: Create store listing assets and metadata

tasks.title: Write App Store and Play Store descriptions with ASO keywords
acceptance_criteria: App Store description ≤4000 characters with keyword density for 'paced reading', 'ADHD reading', 'focus reading'
Play Store short description ≤80 characters
Play Store full description ≤4000 characters
Zero ableist language (no 'suffering from', 'overcoming disability', 'normal reading')
Positioning as completion app, not speed-reading app
No manipulative urgency language
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: store descriptions are one copywriting deliverable per platform — parallelizable with privacy policy, no code dependency

tasks.title: Create privacy policy documenting on-device-only data handling
acceptance_criteria: Privacy policy states all reading content stays on device
Explicitly states no cloud upload without per-session opt-in
Lists what data is collected (analytics on-device only)
Complies with GDPR and CCPA minimum requirements
Written in plain language (no legal jargon without explanation)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: privacy policy is one legal document reflecting on-device-only data handling — parallelizable with store descriptions

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: store descriptions and privacy policy — independent documents with no shared content enable parallel writing

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: store listing content is copywriting and legal documentation — no code changes, distinct from Fastlane CI/CD configuration in F1.5.2.2

features.title: Internal Testing Setup

stories.title: Configure TestFlight and Play Store internal testing tracks

tasks.title: Configure Play Store internal testing track via Fastlane
acceptance_criteria: Fastlane has 'internal' lane that uploads to Play Store internal testing track
Lane uses supply action with track: 'internal'
No signing secrets hardcoded (uses environment variables)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: Play Store Fastlane internal lane is one config addition to android/fastlane/Fastfile — parallelizable with iOS TestFlight config

tasks.title: Configure TestFlight upload via Fastlane
acceptance_criteria: Fastlane has 'beta' lane that uploads to TestFlight
Lane uses pilot action
No signing secrets hardcoded (uses environment variables)
tasks.granularity_rationale: Atomic: TestFlight Fastlane beta lane is one config addition to ios/fastlane/Fastfile — parallelizable with Android Play Store config

stories.granularity_rationale: Story groups 2 tasks: Play Store and TestFlight Fastlane configs — platform-independent lane definitions enable parallel configuration

features.granularity_rationale: Feature split: internal testing setup modifies Fastlane lane definitions for iOS and Android — platform-specific distribution pipeline configs

epics.granularity_rationale: Epic scope: app store presence spans platform-specific Fastlane configs, legal/privacy documents, and marketing copy — parallelizable with E1.5.1 due to zero file overlap

parallel_clusters.rationale: Stats foundation and store presence have no file overlap
Android and iOS Fastlane configs are independent

milestones.granularity_rationale: Milestone boundary: launch readiness is the final validation gate — store listing requires feature-complete app for screenshots, and stats require completed reading sessions to measure

releases.granularity_rationale: Release boundary: MVP establishes core product for App Store submission — complete reading experience, content ingestion pipeline, and store presence required before market validation
